data.title: Mesa Air and Republic Airways are expected to close their merger on November 25
data.description:
The merger between Mesa Air Group (MESA) and Republic Airways is set to close on November 25 following a reverse 1-for-15 stock split of Mesa Air shares. 

The merger will create one of the largest regional airline operators in the U.S. Upon closing, the newly combined entity will be named Republic Airways Holdings Inc. and will trade publicly on Nasdaq under the symbol. The post-merger airline will operate roughly 310 Embraer 170/175 jets, with more than 1,250 daily departures, serving routes through agreements with American Airlines, Delta Air Lines, and United Airlines.

All outstanding Mesa debt will be extinguished in the transaction, leaving the company with a strong financial position and enhanced scale. Mesa (MESA) shareholders will own between 6% and 12% of the post-merger company, depending on achieving certain pre-close criteria. Republic shareholders will own at least 88%. Crucially, Mesa will retain its partnership with United Airlines (UAL) under a new 10-year capacity agreement, while Republic continues services for major network carriers.

The integration is designed to benefit from increased economies of scale and financial flexibility, targeting approximately $1.9 billion in annual revenue, robust cash flow, and improved margins. The deal is seen by analysts as positioning the combined company for enhanced stability, greater investment capacity, and strategic agility within the competitive U.S. regional airline market.

data.title: AnaptysBio drops after litigation with GSK unit risks cancer therapy deal
data.description:
AnaptysBio (ANAB) shares lost ~11% in the premarket on Friday after the U.S. drugmaker and its partner Tesaro, a subsidiary of GSK (GSK), sued each other over a licensing and collaboration agreement related to their jointly developed cancer drug Jemperli (dostarlimab).

San Diego, California-based AnaptysBio (ANAB) said it has filed a lawsuit in Delaware Chancery Court alleging that Tesaro, influenced by GSK (GSK), has violated their exclusive agreement signed in 2014.

At stake are hundreds of millions of dollars in payments that AnaptysBio (ANAB) is entitled to receive from GSK (GSK) as part of the collaboration under which the U.K. drugmaker is developing Jemperli as monotherapy and in combination with other drugs for various tumors.

Meanwhile, GSK (GSK) announced that Tesaro has taken legal action in the same court, accusing AnaptysBio (ANAB) of breaching the agreement after the immunology drugmaker threatened to revoke Tesaro’s license for Jemperli due to alleged violations of the deal.

“The breach entitles TESARO to terminate the current license agreement, obtain a perpetual and irrevocable license to dostarlimab, and to reduce the royalties and milestone payments due by TESARO to AnaptysBio by 50%,” the company said. 

A trial for the lawsuit is expected to get underway in July 2026.

data.title: VinFast Auto GAAP EPS of -$0.41 misses by $0.12, revenue of $718.63M; maintains FY25 target
data.description:
• VinFast Auto press release (VFS): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$0.41 misses by $0.12.
  • Vehicle sales were VND 16,695.2 billion (US$662.9 million) in the third quarter of 2025, representing an increase of 48.4% from the third quarter of 2024 and an increase of 11.4% from the second quarter of 2025.
  • Revenue of $718.63M, representing an increase of 46.8% from the third quarter of 2024.
    EV deliveries were 38,195 in the third quarter of 2025, representing a 7% increase quarter-over-quarter and a 74% increase year-over-year. Cumulatively, in the first nine months of 2025, the Company delivered 110,362 EVs to customers globally, representing a 149% increase year-over-year.
      ● E-scooter and e-bike deliveries were 120,052 in the third quarter of 2025, representing a 73% increase quarter-over-quarter and a 535% increase year-over-year. Cumulatively, in the first nine months of 2025, the Company delivered 234,536 e-scooters and e-bikes, representing a 489% increase year-over-year.
      ● As of September 30, 2025, customers can purchase VinFast EVs from 381 showrooms globally

  • Business Outlook

      ● FY2025 Delivery Target: The Company maintains its target to at least double its global EV deliveries in 2025.
      ● The Company expects the growth momentum in the e-scooter business to strengthen in the coming months, driven by favorable government policies promoting two-wheeler (2W) electrification in Vietnam and the Company’s ongoing efforts to accelerate this transition.

data.title: GFL holders offer 16.6M subordinate voting shares at $45.15 each
data.description:
• GFL Environmental (GFL) said that several shareholders — including BCEC-GFL Borrower (Cayman) LP, Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board, GFL Borrower II (Cayman) LP, and entities affiliated with HPS Investment Partners — are selling 16.6 million GFL subordinate voting shares in a secondary offering at $45.15 per share.
  • RBC Capital Markets and RBC Dominion Securities are underwriting the sale in the U.S. and Canada, respectively.
  • GFL also agreed to purchase for cancellation a total of 1,275,000 Shares from RBC Dominion Securities under the offering.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Buckle GAAP EPS of $0.96 beats by $0.01, revenue of $320.84M beats by $2.87M
data.description:
• Buckle press release (BKE): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.96 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $320.84M (+9.3% Y/Y) beats by $2.87M.
  • Comparable store net sales for the 13-week fiscal quarter ended November 1, 2025 increased 8.3 percent from comparable store net sales for the prior year 13-week period ended November 2, 2024.
  • Online sales increased 13.6 percent to $53.0 million for the 13-week fiscal quarter ended November 1, 2025, compared to net sales of $46.6 million for the 13-week fiscal quarter ended November 2, 2024.

data.title: BJ’s Wholesale Club tops Q3 estimates; updates FY outlook
data.description:
• BJ’s Wholesale Club press release (BJ): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.16 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $5.35B (+4.9% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Comparable club sales increased by 1.1% year-over-year.
  • Comparable club sales, excluding gasoline sales, increased by 1.8% year-over-year, with a two-year stack of 5.5%.
  • Membership fee income increased by 9.8% year-over-year to $126.3 million.
  • Digitally enabled comparable sales growth was 30%, reflecting two-year stacked comp growth of 61%.

  • The Company provided an updated outlook for fiscal 2025:

    • Comparable club sales, excluding the impact of gasoline sales, to increase 2.0% to 3.0% year-over-year.
    • Adjusted EPS to range from $4.30 to $4.40 (prior $4.20 to $4.35) vs $4.33 consensus.
    • Capital expenditures of approximately $800 million.

data.title: Post Holdings sees a boost from acquisitions in FQ4
data.description:
Post Holdings (POST) reported sales rose 11.8% year-over-year in its fiscal fourth quarter to $2.25 billion. The tally included $249.4 million in sales churned up from acquisitions in the current year period. 

Excluding the benefit from acquisitions, sales growth in the Foodservice segment (primarily driven by incremental highly pathogenic avian influenza pricing and volume growth in eggs and protein-based shakes), Weetabix segment (primarily driven by favorable foreign currency exchange rates), and the Refrigerated Retail segment (primarily driven by incremental highly pathogenic avian influenza pricing) was offset by declines in the Post Consumer Brands segment (driven by pet food distribution losses and cereal category declines). 

Gross profit was 26.8% of net sales, compared to 28.6% a year ago, while operating profit fell 11.8% to $190.9 million. The food company reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.09 vs. $1.53 a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA was up 22.0% to $425.4 million.

Looking ahead, Post (POST) expects FY26 adjusted EBITDA to be between $1.5 billion and $1.54 billion (midpoint $1.52 billion), inclusive of a partial year contribution from 8th Avenue's pasta business.

Shares of Post Holdings (POST) were down 1.8% in premarket trading on Friday and trade in the bottom quarter of their 52-week range. Wall Street analysts have a consensus Buy rating on the stock, while Seeking Alpha analysts are more cautious with a consensus Hold rating in place.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs see record $3.79B outflows in November, BTC nears worst month since 2022 - report
data.description:
The 11 U.S.-listed spot bitcoin ETFs have now logged a combined $3.79 billion in outflows in November, the largest on record and exceeding the previous peak of $3.56 billion set in February, CoinDesk reported, citing data from SoSoValue.

BlackRock’s bitcoin ETF (IBIT) has recorded more than $2 billion in redemptions this month, according to SoSoValue. In contrast, the newly launched Solana (SOL-USD) and XRP (XRP-USD) ETFs have attracted $300.46 million and $410 million in net inflows, respectively. Ether ETFs have also faced record outflows, totaling $1.79 billion.

Meanwhile, Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) November rout deepened Friday, with the token plunging below $85,000 amid leveraged wipeouts and souring sentiment—putting it on course for its worst month since the crypto collapse of 2022. Bitcoin sank as much as 6.4% to $81,629, Ether (ETH-USD) dropped 7.6% below $2,700.

Other notable crypto decliners included XRP (XRP-USD) -4.5%, Binance Coin (BNB-USD) -5.8%, Stellar (XLM-USD) -5.2%, Celestia (TIA-USD) -7.2%, Uniswap (UNI-USD) -4.8%, Avalanche (AVAX-USD) -5.2%, Dogecoin (DOGE-USD) -6.7%, and Solana (SOL-USD) -5.8%.

data.title: Azenta Non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.01, revenue of $159M beats by $2.64M; gives FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Azenta press release (AZTA): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $159M (+5.3% Y/Y) beats by $2.64M.
  • Organic revenue, which excludes the impact from foreign exchange, grew 4% year over year, mainly attributable to higher revenue in Multiomics.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $21 million, and Adjusted EBITDA margin was 13.0%, an improvement of 230 basis points year over year.
  • FY'26 organic revenue growth expected to be 3% to 5% year over year, with Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of approximately 300 basis points.

  • Guidance for Full Year Fiscal 2026 

    • Total organic revenue is expected to grow in the range of 3% to 5% relative to fiscal 2025.
    • Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion is expected to be approximately 300 basis points relative to fiscal 2025.

data.title: Belden declares $0.05 dividend
data.description:
• Belden (BDC) declares $0.05/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.19%
  • Payable Jan. 8; to shareholders of record Dec. 11; ex-div Dec. 11.
  • See BDC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
